message:
feat(sharding): upgrade spawn_with_hints_with_cx to route via spawn_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-on enhancement to tick 46's `spawn_with_cx` trait extension.
Before tick 46, `spawn_with_hints_with_cx` could only thread cx
through the internal `pane_routes.write_with_cx` RwLock acquire;
the actual subprocess hop had to use the ambient
`backend.handle.spawn()` because the `WeztermInterface` trait had
no `spawn_with_cx` surface. The old doc comment even called this
out:

  > The `backend.handle.spawn()` call remains ambient (WeztermHandle
  > spawn is CLI-only and out of scope; see ft-xbnl0.2.3 bead for
  > the retry-helper refactor seam).

That constraint is gone. This commit:

  * Swaps `backend.handle.spawn(cwd, domain_name)` for
    `backend.handle.spawn_with_cx(cx, cwd, domain_name)` so caller
    cancellation/budget/virtual time propagate end-to-end through
    the subprocess hop AND the RwLock acquire.

  * Updates the doc comment to reflect the upgrade: the Cx-first
    entry point now threads cx "end-to-end through the entire
    spawn pipeline" (subprocess I/O + pane-route bookkeeping).

Regression test (new)
`spawn_with_hints_with_cx_routes_and_records`:

  * Mirrors the existing non-cx `spawn_with_agent_hint_uses_agent_assignment`
    topology (2 MockWezterm shards, ByAgentType assignment with
    Codex -> shard 1, ClaudeCode -> shard 0).
  * Invokes `client.spawn_with_hints_with_cx(&cx, None, None,
    Some(AgentType::Codex)).await`.
  * Asserts:
    - Agent-hint routing still works: global pane id decodes to
      (ShardId(1), 0).
    - Shard 0 stays at 0 panes, shard 1 goes to 1 pane.
    - `pane_routes.read_with_cx(&cx)` contains an entry for the new
      global pane id mapping to `(ShardId(1), local_pane_id=0)`.

Note: MockWezterm's trait default `spawn_with_cx` delegates to
`spawn`, so on the mock side the subprocess hop is still
observationally equivalent to the non-cx version — the test proves
the Cx-first call chain works end-to-end without regressing the
agent-hint routing logic. For a real WeztermClient backend, the cx
would now additionally propagate through `WeztermClient::spawn_with_cx`
-> `spawn_targeted_with_cx` -> `run_cli_with_cx` -> `timeout_with_cx`
(tick 40-42's helper chain), which is what the tick 40 / tick 42
short-circuit tests already pin at the unit level.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * sharding:: — 96 passed / 0 failed (tick 46: 95 -> tick 47: 96,
    +1 new test)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
